message:
fix(combinator): winner panic priority in race; feat: pipeline! macro; cleanup choreography scaffolds

combinator/race.rs: Fix panic-priority bug in both `race2_to_result` and
`race_all_to_result`. Previously, the winner's Outcome::Panicked was handled
inside a general match block at the same priority level as Ok/Err/Cancelled,
after the loser panic check. Now the winner panic is checked immediately at
the top of each function and returned as RaceError::Panicked before any other
logic. The old Panicked arm in the match is marked unreachable!(), making the
invariant compiler-enforced. This ensures panics (the most severe outcome)
are always surfaced first regardless of which participant panicked.

combinator/pipeline.rs: Implement the pipeline! macro, replacing the
compile_error!() stub. The macro generates an async block that captures the
context by reference and sequentially threads a value through each stage:
`(stage)(cx, value).await`. Each stage's output becomes the next stage's
input. New test verifies sequential chaining: (2 + 3) * 4 = 20.

obligation/choreography/pipeline.rs: Replace todo!() macros in generated lab
test scaffolding with harmless unit values `()`. The old generated code would
panic at runtime if anyone ran the scaffold tests. Channel pair declarations
now emit `let (chan_a, chan_b) = ((), ());` instead of todo!(). Comments
reframed from "TODO" to intentional scaffold documentation. Test assertions
updated to match new generated output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
